page_content:
HLTH371 Communicating Safety and Health (3 Credits)

The communication and evaluation of safety and health information. Emphasis on various types of communications and recipient factors which contribute to their success or failure.

Restriction: Must be in a major within SPHL-Behavioral & Community Health department.

HLTH374 Drugged, Drowsy & Distracted Driving: traffic safety issues for the new millenium (3 Credits)

An in-depth examination of the current status of research, historical trends and policies regarding impaired driving. Designed to provide exposure to the research process for understanding the behavioral factors that contribute to impaired driving in our society.

Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in HLTH200 and HLTH300. Restriction: Must be in Community Health program.
